title: Premium Rose Kaju Katli with Golden Border
description: An elegant layered cashew sweet featuring ivory-white kaju layers, a thin rose-date center, and a decorative golden border. Minimalist luxury with optional rose petal or pistachio garnish.

preparation.instruction: Prepare ingredients and set up workspace.
tips: Ensure all ingredients are measured and ready before starting.
Use a heavy-bottom pan to prevent sugar syrup from burning.
Keep thickness guides handy for rolling sheets uniformly.
subSteps: Measure 650 g fine cashew powder and sift through a fine mesh.
Measure 350 g sugar and 175 ml water in separate containers.
Measure 15 g ghee and 1 g cardamom powder for the white dough.
Measure filling ingredients: 180 g seedless dates, 50 g almond powder, 25 g gulkand, 2 g rose petal powder, 5 g ghee.
Measure coloring: have beta-carotene color ready in small container.
Set up parchment paper on work surface for rolling.
VALIDATION: All ingredients measured and visible. Workspace clean and organized.

ingredients.name: Ghee
ingredients.notes: 15 g for white dough, 5 g for filling

tips: Do not overcook the syrup — one-string consistency is ideal (syrup drops as a single string from spoon).
Use a candy thermometer if available — aim for 106–110°C.
Stop heating immediately when syrup reaches desired consistency.
subSteps: Pour 350 g sugar and 175 ml water into a heavy-bottom pan.
Heat gently over medium heat, stirring occasionally until sugar dissolves completely.
Once sugar is dissolved, stop stirring and let it cook undisturbed.
Cook until syrup becomes slightly sticky and reaches one-string consistency (approximately 8–10 minutes).
Test consistency: dip a spoon in syrup, let it cool slightly, and pull — it should form a single string.
Remove from heat immediately when ready.
VALIDATION: Syrup flows like honey. One-string test successful. Color is clear and light golden.

tips: Add cashew powder gradually to avoid lumps.
Keep flame low to prevent burning.
The dough is ready when it forms a soft mass and leaves the pan sides.
subSteps: Reduce flame to low.
Gradually add 650 g fine cashew powder to the warm syrup while stirring continuously.
Mix thoroughly, breaking up any lumps that form.
Keep stirring constantly for 8–10 minutes until dough comes together.
The dough is ready when: it leaves the sides of the pan, forms a soft cohesive mass, and does not stick excessively.
Add 15 g ghee and 1 g cardamom powder.
Mix well and remove from heat immediately.
Target temperature: 80–85°C (warm but not hot to handle).
VALIDATION: Dough is smooth and creamy. No lumps visible. Cardamom aroma is evident. Temperature allows comfortable handling.

tips: Work quickly while dough is warm — it becomes difficult to divide once cooled.
Keep unused portions covered to prevent drying.
Beta-carotene should dissolve in warm water first for even color distribution.
subSteps: Divide warm dough into two portions: 85% (approximately 860 g) for white sheets and 15% (approximately 155 g) for golden border.
Keep white dough covered with a damp cloth.
Dissolve beta-carotene color in 2–3 drops of warm water to create a thin paste.
Add colored water to reserved 155 g dough.
Knead the golden dough thoroughly until color is uniform and even throughout.
Target shade: warm golden yellow similar to marigold flower — not bright lemon yellow.
Cover golden dough immediately with plastic wrap.
VALIDATION: White dough is smooth and ivory-colored. Golden dough is uniformly colored marigold-gold throughout. Both portions are warm and pliable.

tips: Cook the filling until firm and non-sticky for easier rolling.
Gulkand and rose petal powder should be added at the end to preserve their aroma.
Allow filling to cool slightly before rolling for better handling.
subSteps: Blend 180 g seedless dates into a smooth paste using a food processor.
Heat 5 g ghee in a small pan over medium heat.
Add date paste and cook, stirring, for 3–4 minutes until ghee is absorbed.
Add 50 g almond powder and mix thoroughly until well incorporated.
Continue cooking and stirring for 2 minutes.
Add 25 g gulkand and 2 g rose petal powder.
Cook, stirring constantly, until mixture becomes: firm, smooth, and non-sticky (approximately 3–4 minutes).
Remove from heat and cool slightly (still warm but safe to handle).
Final state: dark reddish-brown, pliable, and easy to roll.
VALIDATION: Filling is firm and no longer sticky. Color is deep reddish-brown. Mixture rolls easily between palms without breaking.

tips: Use thickness guides (3 mm) for consistency.
Work on parchment paper to prevent sticking.
Keep unused dough covered to prevent drying.
subSteps: Divide white dough into 2 equal portions (approximately 430 g each).
Take first portion and place on parchment paper.
Using a rolling pin with 3 mm thickness guides, roll into a smooth square sheet.
Target size: 8 x 8 inches (20 x 20 cm).
Cut edges neatly with a sharp knife.
Transfer to clean parchment paper.
Repeat with second portion of white dough.
VALIDATION: Both sheets are 3 mm thick, 8 x 8 inches, smooth surface, uniform color. Edges are clean and straight.

steps.instruction: Roll rose-date filling and golden border.
tips: Keep golden dough warm and covered — it becomes difficult to roll if it cools.
Filling sheet should be slightly smaller than white sheets to allow margin.
Golden strips should be straight and of uniform width.
subSteps: Place rose-date filling on parchment paper.
Using a rolling pin with 2 mm thickness guides, roll into a square sheet.
Target size: 8 x 8 inches (20 x 20 cm) — this will have margins when placed between white sheets.
Keep on parchment paper.
Take golden dough and roll into a thin sheet using 2–3 mm guides.
Cut into long strips of 8 mm width and 2–3 mm thickness.
Keep strips warm and covered with plastic wrap until assembly.
VALIDATION: Filling sheet is 2 mm thick and 8 x 8 inches. Golden strips are uniform in width (8 mm) and thickness (2–3 mm). All pieces are warm and pliable.

tips: Leave 2 mm margins around the filling for clean presentation.
Press gently — do not compress heavily as this affects texture.
Keep assembled slab on parchment paper.
subSteps: Place first white kaju sheet on clean parchment paper.
Carefully place rose-date filling sheet on top.
Position filling to leave approximately 2 mm margin around all edges.
Place second white kaju sheet on top of filling.
Press gently with palms to adhere layers — do not compress heavily.
VALIDATION: Three layers are visible: white bottom, rose-date center (slightly inset), white top. Layers are bonded but not compressed. Slab is approximately 8 mm thick.

tips: Use thin adhesive to prevent soaking the delicate layers.
Work quickly around the perimeter.
Trim corners neatly for professional appearance.
subSteps: Prepare light adhesive: either thin sugar syrup or thin cashew paste.
Using a pastry brush, lightly brush the exposed perimeter (all four edges) of the assembled slab.
Apply golden strips around all four sides, pressing gently as you go.
Trim excess at corners and cut corners clean.
Allow adhesive to set for 2–3 minutes.
VALIDATION: Golden border is adhered evenly around all four sides. Corners are clean and neatly trimmed. Border is uniform in width.

steps.instruction: Rest and cut the kaju katli.
tips: Do not refrigerate during resting — room temperature setting allows proper texture development.
Use a sharp knife and clean between cuts for neat edges.
Cut into uniform sizes for premium presentation.
subSteps: Place assembled slab on clean parchment paper.
Cover loosely (do not seal tightly as condensation can affect surface).
Allow to rest at room temperature for 2–3 hours until firm but still slightly pliable.
Test firmness: slab should not break when gently pressed but should feel solid.
Using a sharp knife, cut slab into uniform pieces: either 1.5 inch squares (retail format) or 1.25 inch diamonds (luxury gift format).
Clean knife blade between each cut for neat edges.
Arrange cut pieces on a clean tray.
VALIDATION: Slab is firm enough to cut cleanly but not brittle. Cut pieces are uniform in size and shape. Edges are clean and sharp. Visible layers are evident in cross-section.

tips: Keep garnish minimal for elegant presentation.
Choose one garnish option for consistency.
Apply garnish gently to avoid disturbing surface.
subSteps: Choose garnish option: dried rose petal fragment, slivered pistachio, or edible gold sprinkle.
Apply one tiny piece per kaju katli piece.
Place garnished pieces on clean serving tray or packing tray.
Allow final garnish to set for 10–15 minutes before packaging.
VALIDATION: Each piece has minimal, elegant garnish. Surface remains smooth and unblemished. Presentation is premium and refined.

beta-carotene-color.notes: Optional; can use silver varak on border instead of golden border

seo.metaTitle: Rose Kaju Katli Recipe | Premium Layered Cashew Sweet
seo.metaDescription: Elegant layered cashew sweet with ivory kaju, rose-date center, and golden border. Premium homemade kaju katli perfect for gifting and festive occasions.
